question_text: Let 𝑋1, 𝑋2, … , 𝑋𝑛 be a random sample of size 𝑛 from a population having uniform distribution over the interval ( 3 , 𝜃), where 𝜃> 3 is an unknown parameter. If 𝑌= max{𝑋1, 𝑋2, … , 𝑋𝑛}, then which one of the following statements is true?

options.A: 𝑛+1 𝑛) (𝑌− 3) + 3 is an unbiased estimator of 𝜃
options.B: 𝑛+1) (𝑌− 3) + 3 is an unbiased estimator of 𝜃
options.C: 𝑛+1 𝑛) (𝑌+ 3) − 3 is an unbiased estimator of 𝜃
options.D: 𝑌 is an unbiased estimator of 𝜃
